A SHORT-LIVED FUNNEL CLOUD WAS REPORTED IN A CUMULUS CONGESTUS CLOUD LINE IN THE VICINITY OF CARYSFORT REEF BY A TRAINED SPOTTER. NO SPRAY RING WAS SEEN...AND THE CIRCULATION LIKELY WAS VERY WEAK.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED JUST EAST OF THE OCEAN REEF YACHT BASIN AROUND 1210 THIS AFTERNOON. THIS WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED UNDER THE ISLAND CLOUD LINE...WAS VERY THIN AND HAD NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING.

OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE CALLED IN A WATERSPOUT. FROM THE UPPER AIR TOWER AN ON DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE COULD SEE THE SPOUT. FOR THE NEXT 45 MINUTES 5 WATERSPOUTS WOULD FORM AND DECAY. TWO SPOUTS WERE ELEPHANT TRUNKS, ONE MEDIUM SPOUT, AND TWO ROPE SPOUTS. SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE FOR 3 OF THE SPOUTS AND ALL WENT THROUGH VARYING TILTS AND COLORS. ALL SPOUTS FORMED AHEAD OF A RAIN SHAFT AND WERE EVENTUALLY CONSUMED BY THEM. ALL SPOUTS MOVED WEST AT 3-5 MPH.
REPORTED BY PUBLIC ON FACEBOOK. WATERSPOUT OCCURRED JUST OUSIDE THE ENTRANCE TO BOOT KEY HARBOR.
42 KT GUST MEASURED AT CWOP STATION.
SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT OFF THE NORTHERN TIP OF SUGARLOAF KEY WHICH WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A BUILDING CUMULUS LINE ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE LOWER KEYS.

MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS REPORTED BY PUBLIC WITH MINIMUM DURATION OF 5 MINUTES ASSOCIATED WITH A RAPIDLY DEVELOPING CUMULUS LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS. PUBLIC DESCRIBED THE WATERSPOUTS AS THIN AND STRAIGHT.
OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT OF 5 MINUTES DURATION. WATERSPOUT REPORTED AS LARGE SIZE.

RECEIVED TWO SPOTTER CALLS REGARDING WATERSPOUTS. A FAMILY OF 3 WATERSPOUTS WERE REPORTED ONE MILE SOUTH OF MILE MARKER 55. THE SECOND CALLER COULD SEE THE HORIZON AND REPORTED THAT THERE WAS NO SPRAY RING WITH THE SPOUTS. HE DESCRIBED TWO ROPES AND ONE ELEPHANT TRUNK THICKNESS. ONE SPOTTER REPORTED A RAIN SHAFT. THE TWO ROPE SPOUTS WERE A LIGHT GREY, AND THE ELEPHANT TRUNK SPOUT A DARK GREY. SPOUTS WERE TILTED GREATER THAN 30 DEGREES, NO LIGHTNING. SPOUTS WERE MOVING WEST AT 5-10 MPH.
PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ONE MILE NORTH OF DUCK KEY VIA FACEBOOK PHOTO.
PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT. WATERSPOUT WAS DISSIPATING DURING PHONE CALL. ESTIMATED DURATION 2 - 3 MINUTES.
AS A THUNDERSTORM MOVED OVERHEAD...THE PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...41 MPH
OFF DUTY WFO KEY WEST EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WELL DEFINED WATERSPOUT. WATERSPOUT WAS BEGINNING TO DISSIPATE DURING PHONE CALL. ESTIMATED DURATION WAS 5 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 42 MPH WAS MEASURED AT AN AUTOMATED WEATHER STATION AT SHARK KEY.

A TRAINED SPOTTER NUMBER 182 OBSERVED A FULLY DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT JUST SOUTH OF SOMBRERO BEACH. ESTIMATED DURATION WAS ABOUT 10 MINUTES BEFORE DISSIPATING.

A SHOE STRING AND UPRIGHT WATERSPOUT WITH A WEAK COLLAR CLOUD AND SPRAY RING WERE OBSERVED.
THE RAMROD CWOP STATION ON RAMROD KEY MEASURED A 38 KNOT WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW.
SPOTTER CALLED TO REPORT A WATERSPOUT HAD DISSIPATED AND REDEVELOPED BETWEEN THE NORTH END OF BIG PINE AND BIG TORCH KEYS. THE SPOTTER SAW THE FUNNEL ON THE WATER WITH A SPRAY RING.
THE DUCK KEY CWOP STATION MEASURED A 40 KNOT OR 46 MPH WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM PASSING THROUGH.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY THE CONTRACT WEATHER OBSERVER AT NAVAL AIR STATION KEY WEST ABOUT 4 MILES NORTH NORTHWEST OF BOCA CHICA FIELD...OR 2 MILES WEST OF SNIPE POINT. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED OVER HALFWAY DOWN FROM THE EASTERN END OF A CUMULONIMBUS BASE AND THE HORIZON.
A WATERSPOUT...THAT WAS INITIALLY SIGHTED AT 656 PM...WAS REPORTED BY THE FLORIDA FISH AND WILDLIFE COMMISSION. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE NORTH AT 5 KNOTS...OR 6 MPH.
A TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED A THIN FUNNEL CLOUD JUST SOUTH OF BAHIA HONDA KEY DRIFTING SLOWLY SOUTHWEST. HE DID NOT OBSERVE A SPRAY RING AND ITS DURATION WAS LESS THAN 5 MINUTES.
THE WEATHERFLOW STATION AT SMITH SHOAL PRODUCED A 34 KNOT WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW.

A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE NORTH-NORTHEAST WAS MEASURED ATOP THE FLORIDA KEYS NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE FORECAST OFFICE AS A GUST FRONT SWEPT THROUGH IN ADVANCE OF AN APPROACHING THUNDERSTORM.

REPORT INCLUDING PHOTO WAS RECEIVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF A WATERSPOUT 5 MILES WEST OF CLUETT KEY. MOVEMENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NORTH AT AN UNKNOWN SPEED. REPORTED DISTANCE BEARING FROM ISLAMORADA IS ESTIMATED.

DELAYED REPORT OF A BRIEF WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES SOUTH OF MARATHON NEAR THE EAST END OF THE 7 MILE BRIDGE.
